Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn map-entry
  "Create a map entry for a key and value pair."
  [k v])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= (key (m/map-entry :a 1)) :a))
- (is (= (val (m/map-entry :a 1)) 1))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn map-entry
  "Create a map entry for a key and value pair."
  [k v]
  #?(:cljs    (cljs.core/MapEntry. k v nil)
     :default (clojure.lang.MapEntry. k v)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn map-kv
  "Maps a function over the key/value pairs of an associative collection. Expects
  a function that takes two arguments, the key and value, and returns the new
  key and value as a collection of two elements."
  [f coll])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= (m/map-kv (fn [k v] [(name k) (inc v)]) {:a 1 :b 2})
- (is (= (m/map-kv (fn [k v] [(name k) (inc v)]) (sorted-map :a 1 :b 2))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn map-kv
  "Maps a function over the key/value pairs of an associative collection. Expects
  a function that takes two arguments, the key and value, and returns the new
  key and value as a collection of two elements."
  [f coll]
  (reduce-map (fn [xf] (fn [m k v] (let [[k v] (f k v)] (xf m k v)))) coll))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn map-keys
  "Maps a function over the keys of an associative collection."
  [f coll])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= (m/map-keys name {:a 1 :b 2})
- (is (= (m/map-keys name (sorted-map :a 1 :b 2))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn map-keys
  "Maps a function over the keys of an associative collection."
  [f coll]
  (reduce-map (fn [xf] (fn [m k v] (xf m (f k) v))) coll))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn filter-kv
  "Returns a new associative collection of the items in coll for which
  `(pred (key item) (val item))` returns true."
  [pred coll])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= (m/filter-kv (fn [k v] (and (keyword? k) (number? v))) {"a" 1 :b 2 :c "d"})
- (is (= (m/filter-kv (fn [k v] (= v 2)) (sorted-map "a" 1 "b" 2))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn filter-kv
  "Returns a new associative collection of the items in coll for which
  `(pred (key item) (val item))` returns true."
  [pred coll]
  (reduce-map (fn [xf] (fn [m k v] (if (pred k v) (xf m k v) m))) coll))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn remove-kv
  "Returns a new associative collection of the items in coll for which
  `(pred (key item) (val item))` returns false."
  [pred coll])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= (m/remove-kv (fn [k v] (and (keyword? k) (number? v))) {"a" 1 :b 2 :c "d"})
- (is (= (m/remove-kv (fn [k v] (= v 2)) (sorted-map "a" 1 "b" 2))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn remove-kv
  "Returns a new associative collection of the items in coll for which
  `(pred (key item) (val item))` returns false."
  [pred coll]
  (filter-kv (complement pred) coll))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn queue?
  "Returns true if x implements clojure.lang.PersistentQueue."
  [x])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- #?(:cljs    (is (m/queue? cljs.core.PersistentQueue.EMPTY))
- :default (is (m/queue? clojure.lang.PersistentQueue/EMPTY)))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn queue?
  "Returns true if x implements clojure.lang.PersistentQueue."
  [x]
  (instance? #?(:cljs    cljs.core/PersistentQueue
                :default clojure.lang.PersistentQueue) x))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn boolean?
  "Returns true if x is a boolean."
  [x])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (m/boolean? true))
- (is (m/boolean? false))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn boolean?
  "Returns true if x is a boolean."
  [x]
  #?(:cljs    (or (true? x) (false? x))
     :default (instance? Boolean x)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn abs
  "Returns the absolute value of a number."
  [x])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= (m/abs -3) 3))
- (is (= (m/abs 2) 2))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn abs
  "Returns the absolute value of a number."
  [x]
  (if (neg? x) (- x) x))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn deref-reset!
  "Sets the value of the atom without regard for the current value, then returns
  the original value of the atom. See also: [[deref-swap!]]."
  [[deref-swap!]])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= (m/deref-reset! a 3) 0))
- (is (= (m/deref-reset! a 1) 3))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn deref-reset!
  "Sets the value of the atom without regard for the current value, then returns
  the original value of the atom. See also: [[deref-swap!]]."
  [atom newval]
  (deref-swap! atom (constantly newval)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn ex-message
  "Returns the message attached to the given Error/Throwable object. For all
  other types returns nil. Same as `cljs.core/ex-message` except it works for
  Clojure as well as ClojureScript."
  [ex])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= (m/ex-message (ex-info "foo" {})) "foo"))
- (is (= (m/ex-message ex) "bar"))))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn ex-message
  "Returns the message attached to the given Error/Throwable object. For all
  other types returns nil. Same as `cljs.core/ex-message` except it works for
  Clojure as well as ClojureScript."
  [ex]
  #?(:clj  (when (instance? Throwable ex) (.getMessage ^Throwable ex))
     :cljr (when (instance? Exception ex) (.-Message ^Exception ex))
     :cljs (cljs.core/ex-message ex)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn ex-cause
  "Returns the cause attached to the given ExceptionInfo/Throwable object. For
  all other types returns nil. Same as `cljs.core/ex-cause` except it works for
  Clojure as well as ClojureScript."
  [ex])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= (m/ex-cause (ex-info "foo" {} cause)) cause))
- :default [(is (= (m/ex-cause (Exception. "foo" cause)) cause))])))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn ex-cause
  "Returns the cause attached to the given ExceptionInfo/Throwable object. For
  all other types returns nil. Same as `cljs.core/ex-cause` except it works for
  Clojure as well as ClojureScript."
  [ex]
  #?(:clj  (when (instance? Throwable ex) (.getCause ^Throwable ex))
     :cljr (when (instance? Exception ex) (.-InnerException ^Exception ex))
     :cljs (cljs.core/ex-cause ex)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn uuid?
  "Returns true if the value is a UUID."
  [x])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (m/uuid? x))
- (is (not (m/uuid? 2)))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn uuid?
  "Returns true if the value is a UUID."
  [x]
  (instance? #?(:clj  java.util.UUID
                :cljr System.Guid
                :cljs cljs.core/UUID) x))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn uuid
  "Returns a UUID generated from the supplied string. Same as `cljs.core/uuid`
  in ClojureScript, while in Clojure it returns a `java.util.UUID` object."
  [s])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (m/uuid? x))
- (is (not (m/uuid? 2)))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn uuid
  "Returns a UUID generated from the supplied string. Same as `cljs.core/uuid`
  in ClojureScript, while in Clojure it returns a `java.util.UUID` object."
  [s]
  #?(:clj  (java.util.UUID/fromString s)
     :cljr (System.Guid. s)
     :cljs (cljs.core/uuid s)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn random-uuid
  "Generates a new random UUID. Same as `cljs.core/random-uuid` except it works
  for Clojure as well as ClojureScript."
  [])

Observed in repo tests:
- related test file: test/medley/core_test.cljc

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn random-uuid
  "Generates a new random UUID. Same as `cljs.core/random-uuid` except it works
  for Clojure as well as ClojureScript."
  []
  #?(:clj  (java.util.UUID/randomUUID)
     :cljr (System.Guid/NewGuid)
     :cljs (cljs.core/random-uuid)))
